entry.title: ABAP Environment in BTP: Handling API Timeout for Long-Running Requests
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have developed an ABAP program in the ABAP Environment on SAP BTP that calls an external API. The request works fine when the response time is short, but when the request takes longer, I get a timeout error:

"The operation was timeout."

I would like to know:

  1. Is it possible to increase the timeout duration for HTTP requests in the ABAP Environment on BTP?
  2. If so, how can I configure it properly?
  3. Are there alternative solutions to handle long-running API requests in ABAP?

Here is a simplified version of my HTTP request implementation:

 

 

   DATA(lo_destination) = cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_url( c_api_endpoint ).
  DATA(lo_client) = cl_web_http_client_manager=>create_by_http_destination( lo_destination ).
....
DATA(lo_response) = lo_client->execute( i_method = if_web_http_client=>post ).

 

 

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

SAP BTP, ABAP environment  API

entry.title: SAP PP: Production Confirmation - Intermediate posting for defective units
entry.content:
Greetings SAPians!

We have following scenario, and I am not clear how to map this requirement.

The Routing consists of more than 10 operations. In between many Production Operations, there are Quality Operations. Quality employee conducts inspection during these Q-operations, posts operation confirmation with only defect-free quantity as Confirmed Yield.

For e.g. (see image below) if Order quantity is 10 PC and at an operation (0350) 5 PC were found to be defective, the Q-employee confirms only 5 good quality units as confirmed yield to next operation and defective units are seen under 'Confirmed scrap' column. The issue is this scrap quantity is not posted anywhere. I mean, no material document is generated for these 5 defective units.

 

The Q-employee creates a Q-notification and physically moves defective units in Blocked Storage, and the decision to rework/scrap the defective parts is taken later after detailed inspection; sometimes even after coordination with Customer, which takes weeks or even months. Hence, from system point-of-view, these 5 defective units remain booked nowhere for evaluation.

The business requirement is to able to see the defective quantity, in which (blocked) storage location they are stored, what is the value of those defective units (process + material consumption costs incurred until defect identification).

Furthermore, at a later date, if it is decided to scrap the defective units, the Q-employee should be able to scrap from Q-notification-Action list (Scrapping Function module).

My primary concern is how to post these defective units to stock. Is there any movemnt type intened for such a posting? Or can I create and assign an intermediate material in BoM and in case of defects, post the defect quantity with movement type 531 in valuated stock with respect to Production Order?

Has anyone encountered and realized such requirement? Any inputs would be appreciated. Thankyou in advance.

SAP ERP, add-on for embedded production planning and detailed scheduling

entry.title: From Revenue and Demand Planning to MRP Production Planning – A technical view
entry.content:
As a follow-up of the functional overview, this article on the topic is the next one in the serie, focusing on how revenue planning and volume/demand planning in SAP Analytics Cloud can integrate with Material Requirement Planning (MRP) process and production planning in SAP S/4HANA, from the technical perspective.

 

From Sales Revenue planning ... 

As explained in the functional article, SAP Analytics Cloud is a real platform capable of dealing with any type of planning processes. For instance, Sales Revenue (as part of a larger Financial Planning process) could be adjusted using drivers if needed, and be represented by different granularity levels (Products, Regions, Time and Version), as per below screenshot. And in this screenshot below, we simply adjust the overall Sales Revenue target for 2025 and Product New Ebike (ID: P_EBIKE).

Sales Revenue targets

Now that Financial Targets have been inputted for our New Ebike product, let's approach Sales Planning from a more supply chain perspective, by converting it into Sales Volume or Sales Quantity. There are various methods to achieve this, but let's consider a straightforward example where each product has a predetermined price for each region (that could also be simulated if necessary). The derivation logic involves calculating the sales volume using the formula Volume = Sales Revenue / Price.

Sales Volume generated from Sales Revenue targets

 It can also be visualized as as Heatmap chart with prior year comparison.

Sales Volume Heat map chart

 

... to Volume/Demand planning ... 

The above Sales Volume now serves as the starting point for the Demand propagation, based on the Manufacturing network. In this example, we have one manufacturing location per region, which is maintained as an attribute of the Region dimension, and we use it to generate the Production Volume per plant.

Production Volume per plant

This setup now acts as the foundation for transferring data to the Planned Independent Requirement (PIR) API, which can be triggered by clicking the "Transfer PIR" button above. To initiate this process, we first select our New EBike product, then click the button. Technically, this button passes the selected line to a Webservice (using JSON) developed on the Business Technology platform, which then maps its content to the fields of the PIR API.

Transfer to SAP S/4HANA as a Planned Independent Requirement

Next, we switch to SAP S/4HANA and open the Display PIRs App to verify that our Planned Independent Requirement has been successfully transferred.

Display PIRs App

We select the different fields.

Field selection based on SAP Analytics Cloud input.

The result displays the exact same values as in SAP Analytics Cloud, but now they are represented as a Planned Independent Requirement within SAP S/4HANA.

Display PIR table

Within SAP S/4HANA, we can now fully run the MRP process for our New eBike product and generate planned orders accordingly, using the Manage Material Coverage App.

Manage Material Coverage App

Result after MRP Run - Planned orders generated

Conclusion

In summary, using SAP's suites of application like SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP Business Technology Platform services, helps businesses streamline their planning and production processes. By accurately forecasting revenue and demand, and then aligning these forecasts with production plans, companies can reduce delays, avoid having too much inventory, and meet customer needs more effectively.

As highlighted previously, this approach is especially beneficial for small to medium-sized businesses, while larger companies might also use SAP Integrated Business Planning for even better results. Overall, this integrated planning process ensures efficient operations and better decision-making, giving businesses a competitive advantage.

Components

  • SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAP BTP Integration Suite
  • Planned Independent Requirement API
  • Planned Independent Requirement Help

entry.title: AI@FRE (Part 5) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI for People and Team
entry.content:
### AI@FRE Blog Series:

AI@FRE (Part 1) - Enabling Enterprise AI Adoption through the BTP FRE Engagement Model
AI@FRE (Part 2) - Accelerating Business AI Transformation with the FRE Value Framework
AI@FRE (Part 3) – Guardrails for Enterprise AI Adoption
AI@FRE (Part 4) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI Architecture Guardrails
AI@FRE (Part 5) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI for People and Team
AI@FRE (PART 6) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – Establish AI Ethics, Security & Privacy

###

Adopting AI in the enterprise goes beyond implementing technology; it requires a people-centered approach. Organizations that successfully integrate AI empower their teams with new skills, redefine roles, and foster a collaborative environment where AI becomes a strategic enabler rather than a disruptor. This blog explores two essential steps for AI adoption: embracing AI within the organization and building robust, AI-capable teams. 

Embracing and Onboarding AI in the Workplace 

For AI to become part of the enterprise fabric, employees need to feel supported, informed, and engaged. Moving AI from a novelty to a standardized tool requires careful alignment with organizational goals and a focus on continuous learning. 

Define the Challenge 

Many employees are apprehensive about AI, often viewing it as complex or a potential threat to job security. Overcoming these perceptions involves: 

  • Effective Communication and Training: Employees must understand how AI can assist rather than replace them. 
  • Empowering Employees: Provide tools and support to integrate AI into everyday tasks and processes. 
  • Cultivating a Culture of Innovation: Encourage experimentation, learning from mistakes, and collaborative exploration of AI’s potential across roles. 

Approach 

  • Communicate and Align: Clearly communicate AI’s capabilities and align them with organizational goals. This ensures employees understand how AI aligns with the company's vision and how it will enhance their roles. 
  • Identify Use Cases: Identify specific business challenges where AI can make a difference, such as automating repetitive tasks or enabling faster, data-driven decisions. 
  • Pilot Projects: Start small by implementing pilot projects that demonstrate AI’s impact on real tasks. These early successes help build confidence and support for further AI initiatives. 
  • Governance and Ethics: Develop a framework to ensure AI is used responsibly. Considerations around data privacy, fairness, and ethical use are paramount to building trust. 
  • Continuous Improvement: Learn from each AI project to mature AI adoption. Celebrate successes, iterate on learnings, and keep refining AI implementations for improved performance and adoption. 

Outcomes and Follow-Up Actions 

  • Invest in Training and Change Management: Ongoing training is critical to help employees gain new skills and fully understand the role of AI in their jobs. 
  • Augment Human Skills: Use AI to optimize and automate repetitive, tiring tasks, allowing employees to focus on more creative, strategic responsibilities. 
  • Opportunity to Redefine Jobs: With AI handling routine tasks, employees can redefine their roles to work alongside AI, focusing on higher-level problem-solving and innovative projects. 

Building AI Teams, Enhancing Capabilities, and Complementing Skill Sets 

Building a capable AI team requires hiring talent, upskilling existing employees, and integrating AI expertise across departments. By assembling an AI-proficient team, organizations create the foundation needed for strategic AI deployment and continuous improvement. 

Define the Challenge 

Organizations face challenges when trying to integrate AI into existing workflows and business processes. A successful AI team should: 

  • Address Skill Gaps: Existing teams may lack AI expertise, necessitating new hires or targeted training. 
  • Facilitate Seamless AI Integration: AI should align smoothly with various business functions, avoiding disruptions. 
  • Foster a Collaborative Environment: Effective AI adoption requires cross-functional cooperation to leverage AI’s full potential. 

Approach 

  • Talent Acquisition: Recruit professionals with expertise in AI, machine learning, data science, and related fields. Skilled hires strengthen the core AI team, bringing fresh perspectives and knowledge. 
  • Training and Development: Offer training programs for existing employees to enhance AI skills, complementing their current skill sets and fostering adaptability. 
  • Cross-functional Collaboration: Promote collaboration between AI experts and other departments. By pairing domain experts with AI specialists, organizations can drive innovation that’s informed by practical, hands-on knowledge. 
  • Continuous Learning: Encourage a culture of ongoing learning to keep the team updated on AI advancements, emerging tools, and best practices. 
  • Trusted Partnerships and External Collaborations: Establish partnerships with institutions, industry experts, and other AI-specialized organizations to supplement internal expertise, bringing in specialized insights and resources. 

 

Outcomes and Follow-Up Actions 

  • Define an AI Strategy: A proficient team can actively shape the organization’s AI strategy and uphold AI ethics, ensuring responsible AI usage. 
  • Train, Explain, and Sustain AI: The team should be skilled at training AI systems, explaining outcomes to stakeholders, and ensuring continuous monitoring for proper functionality. 
  • Augment AI Skills for Scalability: As the organization scales AI, standardized processes and tools become vital, and an empowered AI team will be prepared to lead these efforts, supporting the wider organization in adopting AI more systematically. 

 

Building a Sustainable AI Culture for the Future 

Creating a sustainable AI culture is an ongoing journey. It begins by fostering AI adoption at the individual and team levels, equipping employees with the tools and training they need to integrate AI into daily operations. AI-powered organizations focus on: 

  1. Empowering Teams: Use AI to relieve employees of repetitive tasks, enabling them to focus on impactful, creative work. 
  1. Continuous Skill Enhancement: Organizations should prioritize training and development to stay at the forefront of AI innovation. 
  1. Purpose-Driven AI Projects: AI should support clear, purpose-driven goals, demonstrating tangible business value and improving organizational agility. 

 

An organization with AI-empowered employees and cross-functional AI teams is better equipped to adapt to market changes, make data-driven decisions, and continuously innovate. By investing in people-first AI adoption, businesses can not only enhance operational efficiency but also create an environment where AI is a valued, collaborative tool for every team member. 

 

Curious on a structured customer engagement model to set up standardized Cloud Architecture for Business AI Adoption? Connect with our Global BTP Customer Engagement Team at BTPFRE@sap.com.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – New options for managing Views for filters tables and charts
entry.content:
Hi @javier_rp1 

app variants can be transported with both customizing as well as workbench requests. Although it makes more sense to use customizing requests which you correctly did.

I believe you're missing a few crucial steps in the general process of creating and transporting app variants, check this documentation. Did you create tile/target mapping (LADI) for you app variant (step 3)? Are you able to open your app variant in your Fiori Launchpad (step 4+5)? After opening your app variant, did you click on the "publish" button (step 6)? 
By clicking on the publish button you're adding the app variant to your selected transport. This does not happen automatically.

Best
Angelika

entry.title: SAP GTS - Legal Changes Hot info
entry.content:
This blog is intended to provide updates on "hot" topics in relation to legal changes affecting the SAP Global Trade Services products. 

This is not a replacement for the official notes released or the the Regulatory Change Manager announcements but rather supplementary information. 

10/02/2025 - AES transition postponement Announcement
The European Commission has postponed the end of the transition period for the AES (Automated Export System) to 12/14/2025.
Certain rules and format specifications apply during this transition period.

SAP Note 3568938 adjusts the determination of the transition period in accordance with the decision of February 7, 2025.

Other Relevant SAP notes.

3569364  SE export: New end of transition phase for AES
3569260  BE export: New end of transition phase for AES

SAP Global Trade Services

entry.title: Re: Custom fields for the App - Reassign Costs and Revenues
entry.content:
Custom fields can only be added in the new version of App - Reassign Costs and Revenues -App ID = F2009A

Please see the following SAP KBA - 

0003549424 - Custom fields is not available in app Reassign Costs and Revenues for Old/New Version - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

and the following - 

The function will be available in the app "Reassign Costs and Revenues - New Version" (F2009A) from version CE2502.

You can find further details on the Help portal - 

App Extensibility: Reassign Costs and Revenues (Key User)

Thanks,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA on-premise extensibility using BTP -- is ABAP...
entry.content:
Hi Mel,

From a strict extensibility point of view i think most of the use cases can be catered with key user extensibility or by developing some custom logic using RAP.

However if you have to develop side by side extensions, automatically generated Fiori elements or freestyle Fiori apps then SAP BTP ABAP environment will be needed to be provisioned.

You can look at the tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/fiori-tools-rap-create-application..html to see how ABAP environment in BTP is used to generate the Fiori app and deploy to on-prem Fiori launchpad.

 

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
Welcome to my first blog post for Asset Management, where I will share the latest updates from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. In the collection below, you will find innovations designed to streamline operations and enhance the user experience, with improvements in the Maintenance Management area.

Table of Contents

Maintenance Management

  • Perform Maintenance Jobs App Updates (▶️ demo video)
  • Automatic Creation of Maintenance Notifications: New Integration (▶️ demo video)
  • Enhanced Filtering in Maintenance Plans (▶️ demo video)
  • Seamless Data Migration with Custom Fields (▶️ demo video)
  • Display Service Contracts in Technical Object
  • Main Work Center Plant Field in Task List API
  • Item Category BadI in Maintenance Order

SAP Asset Performance Management

More Information / Additional Links 

Before delving into each feature, I invite you to watch the release highlights video below. There, we showcase what is new for Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release.

Now, let's explore in detail the individual innovations in Maintenance Management.

Perform Maintenance Jobs App Updates

Material Barcode Scanning 

The Perform Maintenance Jobs (F5104A) Fiori app has introduced new features designed to improve user interactions.

Maintenance technicians can now scan barcodes for unplanned materials, which eliminates manual entry errors and enhances accuracy. By integrating barcode scanning technology into the maintenance job process, the Material ID is automatically populated during the material scan, saving time during maintenance operations.

This feature is available in both the unplanned components section and the add unplanned component popup.

Enhanced Time Recording

The second update to the Perform Maintenance Jobs app includes an enhancement to the record time feature, which now features a new team filter in the value help. This enhanced value help automatically recognizes the work center team to which a user belongs, pre-filtering the employee lists based on their team assignment when recording time.

This tailored view significantly benefits those managing large teams, as it increases the speed and ease of recording time for team members. By utilizing the team filter, managers and planners can ensure accurate time bookings, which is a crucial aspect of effective workforce management.

Watch the video below in which I show you the enhancements of the Perform Maintenance Jobs app:

Demo 1: Perform Maintenance Jobs Updates: Material Barcode Scanning and Enhanced Time Recording

Automatic Creation of Maintenance Notifications: New Integration

The automatic creation of maintenance notifications for threshold-based measuring points has been available since version 2202 and now integrates with the Perform Maintenance Jobs app (F5104A) and the Measurement Document OData API.

This feature generates notifications automatically when a maintenance technician records a measurement exceeding thresholds. It is vital for reducing unplanned downtimes and ensuring consistent use across maintenance platforms. By addressing issues from threshold breaches, businesses can maintain operational continuity and improve resource allocation, resulting in better service levels and enhanced asset performance.

Watch the demo below to see the integration with the Perform Maintenance Jobs app:

Demo 2: Automatic Creation of Maintenance Notifications: New Integration

Enhanced Filtering in Maintenance Plans

The Manage Maintenance Plans (F5325) Fiori app has been upgraded with advanced filtering capabilities. Users can now filter maintenance plans using criteria such as:

  • plan cycle
  • cycle unit
  • cycle description
  • offset
  • offset unit

This new feature enhances both single-cycle and counter-based maintenance plans, allowing for more efficient planning and reporting. Maintenance teams can now identify specific cycles needing attention, reducing oversights and backlog.

These improvements support proactive maintenance strategies essential for productivity and cost management in asset-intensive industries.

Watch the demo and learn how to find the new filters:

Demo 3: Enhanced Filtering in Maintenance Plans

Seamless Data Migration with Custom Fields

The 2502 release facilitates a smooth migration process for maintenance plans and items from external systems, including custom fields created through key user extensibility. This enhancement allows for the transfer of important customized data into the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition environment while preserving the integrity of historical data.

It is essential for companies transitioning to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to safeguard critical business data and make it easily accessible within the SAP landscape. The automated transfer of custom fields enables businesses to continue their preventive maintenance programs seamlessly, without any data loss.

This ensures compliance with specific business requirements, improves decision-making capabilities, and supports continuity and reliability in operations.

Watch the demo and check how it works in the system:

Demo 4: Seamless Data Migration with Custom Fields

Display Service Contracts in Technical Object

The introduction of the “Service Contract” tab in the Find Technical Object (F2072) Fiori app supports maintenance planners and their need to efficiently review service contracts. This tab provides a consolidated view of all service contracts.

Whether dealing with functional locations or equipment, maintenance planners can now easily access and refer to all associated contract coverages from one location, increasing accessibility and convenience.

The new interface groups service contracts by validity dates, displaying the most recent contracts first, facilitating quick retrieval and analysis.

Image 1: Display Service Contracts in Technical Object

Moreover, the Service Contract tab allows maintenance planners to navigate from the functional location and equipment pages to the service contract details object page, supplementing the user journey with relevant quick links.

This feature emphasizes the interconnected nature of service contracts and technical objects, ensuring that maintenance planners have all the necessary information to hand without leaving the primary application window.

Image 2: Display Service Contracts in Technical Object

Main Work Center Plant Field in Task List API

In 2502, we introduce the "MainWorkCenterPlant" field in the Maintenance Task List API. Previously, there was a discrepancy between the Order APIs and Task List API: the Order APIs used the "MainWorkCenterPlant" field, while the Task List API used "Plant". By standardizing the field name across both APIs, we ensure a more coherent and reliable development environment.

The exposure of "MainWorkCenterPlant" in both the task list header and operations API promises a unified approach to work center plant data management.

Image 3: Main Work Center Plant field in Task list API

The existing "Plant" field remains available for now in the Task List API; however, it is slated for deprecation in future releases. SAP strongly recommends users transition to the "MainWorkCenterPlant" field to avoid potential disruptions when the old field is phased out.

This transition period lets developers adjust their implementations gradually, ensuring applications continue to function seamlessly as SAP updates its offerings.

Item Category BadI in Maintenance Order

The new item category BAdI in maintenance orders, EAM_ITEM_CAT_DET_CNTRL, enables the automatic proposal of item categories in maintenance orders, utilizing all visible fields available in the component and service screens as potential input parameters.

Utilizing the EAM_ITEM_CAT_DET_CNTRL BAdI, it is now possible to customize item category determination directly within the materials tab of the Maintenance Order. Through custom logic, users can fine-tune item category proposals, enabling more accurate and context-sensitive defaults.

Image 4: Item category BadI in Maintenance Order

SAP Asset Performance Management 

SAP Asset Performance Management empowers asset owners, plant managers, and reliability engineers to optimize maintenance strategies, measure and improve asset performance, and follow through on your sustainability objectives.

Stay tuned for the blog post from Kate Dowle where she will share the latest updates on APM.

 

More Information / Additional Links 

You will find a collection of SAP S/4HANA Cloud product centric blog posts here:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management – The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Manufacturing & Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • Discrete Industries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - The Link Collection

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up-to-date to our latest enablement assets: Fernanda Rodrigues | LinkedIn

entry.title: SAP TM BOPF Navigator -Transaction /SCMTMS/BONAV
entry.content:
SAP TM BOPF Navigator -Transaction /SCMTMS/BONAV

 

SAP Transportation Management (TM) system includes various tools and interfaces, one of which is the Business Object Processing Framework (BOPF). The BOPF Navigator is a powerful tool that visualizes and manages the relationships between different business objects within SAP TM.llows Consultant to navigate between different business objects and explore how they interact with each other. This helps in analyzing the impact of changes in one object on others.

As SAP TM use the BOPF framework and as consultant you need to debug an Action, Determination, Validation, sometimes a request from a developer colleague is needed to review it as SAP TM in front end with Webdynpro you’re no familiar where setup a breakpoint or know which class/method should be use.

The Transaction - /SCMTMS/BONAV is useful to support in search analysis. This transaction gives all the standard TM implementations and the Custom one:

Using this transaction and select “Show Customer Enhancements” allow you select any custom implementation and show the details as bellow:

You can navigate direct to any Action give double click on it, to navigate to the Code or direct setting Breakpoint from outside in the transaction.

About the standard implementations in BOPF we can also view it selecting #SHOW_ALL

By using this one you can select any part which you need to set a breakpoint, i.e Which Select Profile is fetch once I start the Transportation Cockpit.

Setting breakpoint :

Start Transportation Cockpit

We can see the Breakpoint stop and view the name of the Selection profile:

This transaction can support Functional Consultant to check more details the code and implementations.

Refer to:

3195231 - Collection of SAP Notes to help root cause analysis - SAP for Me

Hope this help you!

Thank you and regards.

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: EWM Performance: Bug in TU / HU (DLV) Assignement
entry.content:
A Small Typo That Caused Big Performance Issues in EWM

Once upon a time (October 2024), one of our clients encountered severe performance issues. Naturally, we investigated the problem, and I found it fascinating how a simple copy-paste mistake could have such a significant side effect. Unfortunately, I was too busy at the time to write about it immediately, but now I finally have the chance to share it.

The client experienced terrible performance when mass-processing delivery documents—specifically, during the mass assignment of multiple deliveries to a transportation unit (TU). The process was so slow that it resulted in a timeout short dump.

I generally find performance topics in EWM quite interesting because there's often room for a 10–50% improvement in execution time with minimal effort. This case was no exception: most of the effort was in understanding why the process was so slow. The fix itself was trivial—especially now, since SAP has already released a corresponding OSS Note.

During debugging, we noticed that the TU/Delivery was being read far more times than expected for this kind of operation.

Debugging the Issue

After further investigation, I discovered that a recently implemented cache in the class /SCWM/CL_SR_TUDLV wasn’t functioning as intended. This class is central for reading TU/Delivery assignments, and in our scenario, it was executing read operations for every single item—repeatedly, for all processed items.

The Bug

Here’s the issue:

Not so easy to spot, right? But with SAP’s correction, it becomes clearer:

The Fix

At the time we identified the problem, SAP had not yet released a fix, so we proceeded with a modification to correct the issue ourselves by replacing sy-subrc with sy-tabix.

A simple copy-paste error led to an incorrect index (lv_index_mtdlv), which caused the entire cache mechanism to fail. As a result, the cache table remained empty, preventing any caching from taking place.

Check If Your System Is Affected

If you want to verify whether this issue affects your system, you can check directly in the method /SCWM/CL_SR_TUDLV=>SETUP.

Alternatively, you can check if your system falls within the gap between the following OSS Notes:

2954670 ( Causing Side effect ) Version Components:

Spoiler
101 SAPK-10110INS4CORE
102 SAPK-10208INS4CORE
103 SAPK-10306INS4CORE
104 SAPK-10404INS4CORE
105 SAPK-10502INS4CORE
701 SAPK-70121INSCMEWM
702 SAPK-70226INSCMEWM
712 SAPK-71221INSCMEWM
900 SAPK-90024INSCMEWM
910 SAPK-91020INSCMEWM
920 SAPK-92016INSCMEWM
930 SAPK-93015INSCMEWM
940 SAPK-94014INSCMEWM
950 SAPK-95008INSCMEWM

and OSS 3516727 (Fixing Side effect) Version Components:

Spoiler
102 SAPK-10216INS4CORE
103 SAPK-10314INS4CORE
104 SAPK-10412INS4CORE
105 SAPK-10510INS4CORE
106 SAPK-10607INS4CORE
107 SAPK-10705INS4CORE
108 SAPK-10803INS4CORE
900 SAPK-90027INSCMEWM
930 SAPK-93022INSCMEWM
940 SAPK-94022INSCMEWM
950 SAPK-95016INSCMEWM

entry.title: Re: Predictive Scenario - Time series forecast - More tha...
entry.content: Hi Eric, you are correct, today you can forecast one account at the time. I know there are certain ways to use an account dimension (dimension made of multiple accounts) and this is one of the way you can "work around" this limitation. Regarding equal spreading, you can actually pre-populate the data in the version where you write back the forecasts, if you want to influence the spreading. Say I forecast on the level of Europe in the hierarchy, I would actually pre-define the values for the leaves (France, Italy, Germany) to influence how the values written back for Europe would be spread towards the leaves. I hope this helps, kind regards Antoine

entry.title: Re: System Creating Reservation instead of PO
entry.content:
When you assign a component to a NWA you specify the Procurement Type which controls how the system reacts to this component.  It can create a reservation, PR, etc.

In configuration the Procurement Type is created in OPS8, it's under Project System, Material, Procurement.

The default procurement type is specified in the Network Profile.

Creating a PO directly is not an option, PS can only create PRs directly, you can automate the conversion from PR to PO through MM.

entry.title: SAP CPI Message Mapping Issue: Handling Empty & Null CountryCode with Conditional Mapping
entry.content:
Hi,

I am facing an issue with SAP CPI Message Mapping, where I need to control the creation of a Address field based on the CountryCode. The problem arises when CountryCode is null or empty, causing the mapping to fail.

I have tried various Mapping approaches, including ifWithoutElse(), equals(), exists(), and not(), but I still cannot get the desired output in all cases. I am reaching out to see if anyone has encountered a similar issue or can suggest a working solution.

Expected/Actual Behavior

Expected Behavior

CountryCode (Input)Processed CountryCodeOutput

"NO""NO"false
"SE""SE"true
"" (empty)"Empty"true
null"Empty"true
"US""US"true

This means:

  • The output should always be true unless CountryCode = "NO", in which case it should return false.
  • If CountryCode is null or "" (empty), it might be converted to "Empty" in order to be processed (not sure if this is the most effective approach) but still always return true.

Actual Behavior

  • When CountryCode is null, the mapping either returns null or does not process correctly.
  • The or() function sometimes receives null instead of true/false, causing failures.
  • Using ifWithoutElse() for CountryCode does not always ensure a valid output.
  • equals(CountryCode, "NO") returns null instead of false when CountryCode is missing.

Current Mapping Approach (Not Working)

I have tried the following Message Mapping functions:

  1. Ensure CountryCode is never null before checking "NO"

    • Using ifWithoutElse(Not(Exists(ns0:CountryCode)), "Empty")

  2. Check if CountryCode is "NO"

    • Using equals(ifWithoutElse(), "NO")

  3. Check if CountryCode is "Empty"

    • Using equals(ifWithoutElse(), "Empty")

  4. Use or() to check if CountryCode is "NO" or "Empty"

    • or(equals("NO"), equals("Empty"))

  5. Use Not(or()) to determine if Address should be created

    • Not(or()) → And() → CreateIf()

Issue

  • equals(ifWithoutElse(), "NO") sometimes returns null instead of false.
  • The or() function does not handle null values properly, causing an empty result.
  • Not(or()) is not always returning the expected true/false values.

Alternative Approaches Tried

1. Groovy Script Attempt (Did Not Work)

We attempted a Groovy script to handle null, "NO", and empty values, but encountered:

  • MissingPropertyException when CountryCode is null.
  • The script still produced null outputs instead of true/false.

2. Using fixContexts(), collapseContexts(), and removeContexts()

  • These functions did not ensure CountryCode was always valid before processing.

3. Using mapWithDefault() or copyValue()

  • These functions did not resolve null values correctly before checking "NO".

Questions

  1. How can I correctly ensure that CountryCode is always processed as a valid string (never null) before checking "NO"? (if this is necessary)
  2. Is there a better way to handle the logic in Message Mapping without requiring Groovy?

I have attached a picture of what i have. The input "CountryCode" as well as the "and" function that "CountryCode" has to be connected to with either the value "true" or "false" depending on the rules stated earlier in this post.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Report download to Excel and special characters
entry.content: I download a vendor report into excel. The vendor name contains a 'special' character - an acute accent over the 'e' in the name. Québec- the report shows the name in excel, for me, as it should, however for another user the name shows as QuÃ©bec.  Any ideas on how to rectify this??

entry.title: Null value in Date Picker Custom Widget in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello - Is it possible to show null value in Date Picker as default if nothing is maintained for the selected record?

This is how it should show when I am creating a new record. It works fine when I do this as a first step.

But when I choose to update an existing record, it shows "End Date" from that record, which is perfectly fine. But, when I click on creating a new record, it shows the "End Date" from previous record. I tried to clear the variable which is used to set the date. But, it doesn't help. Any input here will be really helpful.

Thanks,

PS

entry.title: Re: I've got few questions while using process manager.
entry.content:
Good day Joshuaaa,

Thank you for sharing your question via the SAP Community!  To offer further support it is recommended that you create a support case using SAP for Me (log in with S-User ID and password). 

You will need to select the specific SAP Signavio product / component as shared in the example below:
 
System: 
Search for SAP Signavio  
 
Product Area: 
SAP Signavio Process Manager > SAP Signavio Process Manager 
 
Component: 
BPI (Business Process Intelligence) 
---  
BPI-PI (SAP Signavio Process Insights) or 
BPI-SIG (SAP Business Transformation Suite by SAP Signavio) – Product & Topic 
 
You can find more details in the SAP Signavio Onboarding Resource Center. 
 
Kind regards,
 
KantriceD

entry.title: Issue in sending XML Data to Application server
entry.content:
I have a requirement to send  payment XML generated from AL11 as it is Line by Line with custom program to Application server.  I have implemented the below Logic by debugging standard functionality implemented by SAP but the issue with my logic is, it is creating Whole XML as a single line in Application server.

I have gone through the many blogs related to XML but i am unable to find the logic to achieve the same. I don't have any prior knowledge on XML hence i am unable to understand the logic posted in blogs.

Sample requirement XML is attached 

Someone help me by correcting the below logic OR adding extra lines to achieve the same with purpose of  every method to gain some knowledge

"When payment File exists reading and send it to AL11
 "GET XML Data generated from F110 payment Run
    SELECT SINGLE *
      FROM regut
      INTO @DATA(is_regut)
      WHERE laufd = @p_laufd        
        AND laufi = @p_laufi         
        AND zbukr IN @s_zbukr.       
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      CALL FUNCTION 'FUNCTION_IMPORT_INTERFACE'
        EXPORTING
          funcname           = 'SDIXML_DOM_TO_SCREEN'
          inactive_version   = ' '
        TABLES
          exception_list     = l_exceptions_tab
          export_parameter   = l_export_tab
          import_parameter   = l_import_tab
          tables_parameter   = l_tables_tab
        EXCEPTIONS
          error_message      = 1
          function_not_found = 2
          invalid_name       = 3
          OTHERS             = 4.

      READ TABLE l_import_tab WITH KEY parameter = 'ENCODING'. " 1932783
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        ld_new = 'X'.
      ENDIF.

      l_encoding = is_regut-codepage.

      g_iixml = cl_ixml=>create( ).
      i_document = g_iixml->create_document( ).


      IF NOT is_regut-tsnam IS INITIAL.                     "nte1428908

        PERFORM read_data(saplfpaym05)
                          TABLES   gt_xfile
                          USING    is_regut-fsnam
                                   is_regut-tsnam
                                   is_regut
                          CHANGING lp_result
                                   lc_rsts_rc
                                   lc_rsts_msg
                                   ls_fbhandle
                                   gp_temse_laenge.

*                       Reset Codepage if we have both TemSe and File           "nte1423701
        IF is_regut-tsnam <> space AND is_regut-fsnam <> space. "nte1423701
          CLEAR is_regut-codepage.                          "nte1423701
        ENDIF.
        IF is_regut-codepage IS INITIAL.
          PERFORM get_codepage(saplfpaym05) USING is_regut-tsnam
                                         CHANGING is_regut-codepage.
        ENDIF.


        IF is_regut-saprl IS INITIAL.
          PERFORM replace_old_characters(saplfpaym05)
                                                  TABLES gt_xfile
                                                  USING  is_regut-codepage.
        ENDIF.


        LOOP AT gt_xfile INTO wa_xfile.
          CONCATENATE lf_string wa_xfile INTO lf_string IN BYTE MODE.
        ENDLOOP.


        i_streamfactory = g_iixml->create_stream_factory( ).
        istream = i_streamfactory->create_istream_xstring( string =
                                                          lf_string ).
        parser = g_iixml->create_parser( stream_factory = i_streamfactory
                                         istream        = istream
                                         document       = i_document ).

        rc = parser->parse( ).

*     get encoding from file, to display the correct encoding
        IF ld_new = 'X'.
          LOOP AT gt_xfile INTO ls_xfile.
            conv = cl_abap_conv_in_ce=>create(
            encoding = l_encoding
            input = ls_xfile ).
            CALL METHOD conv->read( IMPORTING data = ls_string ).
            EXIT.
          ENDLOOP.
        ENDIF.

      "Write XML to AL11
      PERFORM r98-ouvrir_fichier USING p_fconfb.  "Get File name
      OPEN DATASET w_fic FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE ENCODING DEFAULT.
      IF sy-subrc NE 0.
        MESSAGE e935(38) WITH w_fic.
      ENDIF.
      TRANSFER ls_string TO w_fic.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        MESSAGE s000(38) WITH TEXT-e10.
      ENDIF.
      CLOSE DATASET w_fic.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA planning Cost Center and Allocations cycles

You can use classic GUI transactions and then copy the planning data to ACDOCP. For this, you can use report R_FINS_PLAN_TRANS_CO_ERP_2_S4H.

entry.title: WFA on HANA Dimension
entry.content:
Hi @YSE-1 ,

While validating a custom dimension I have found the following scenario:

I am looking at EOP HC analyzed by custom dimension, 2025 so basically as of today.

I have a user with 2 events happening in EC on the same date, 1st Nov 2024.

First event is a Transfer, which WFA captures.

Second event is a Position Change, which WFA does not capture.

In the second event entry, the user has a change in the source field of the custom dimension.

When analyzing as mentioned above, EOP HC analyzed by custom dimension, 2025 so basically as of today, I get the old value (prior to Position Change) in the custom dimension node.

Why is this and how can I fix it? Even if I do not capture the Position Sync, I still need just 1 event per day in order to capture the correct data? I think that it is an automated event, not even performed manually by someone so it could basically happen to many other users (?!).

Many thanks in advance for your assistance.

Kind regards,

Alex

entry.title: Re: Transfer Manager functionality in Settlement Management
entry.content:
If you needed a transfer rule to work for a settlement type but only when linked to a specific contract type I assume you would need either to create a new settlement type or code it.

Example fixed value rebate - no business volume

Delta accruals we need to use a settlement material to post

But we need a profit centre that comes from the plant view or we need to default one for the accruals.

But this is only required for this specific contract type when delta accruals are posted. 

I assume we would need a different delta accruals settlement type OR a more complex transfer in place to differentiate when we do or do not need to populate the profit centre

entry.title: Changing Default WSS Security SignatureMethod and DigestMethod Algorithms in SAP PI/PO
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm looking at the SOAP adapter (receiver) options for Web Service Security and looking to implement the Sign option  on an outbound XML Payload. However when examining the SignatureMethod and DigestMethod tags, it looks like the default Algorithm is rsa-sha1 and sha1 respectively. However the provider web service requires these to be hmac-sha256 for signing and sha256 for the Digest. Is there any way to override these options and use different signing algos? I'm ok to use either HTTP/SOAP or HTTP/Axis adapters to achieve this. I assume there is a module available that SAP hasn't  published (again) or Java mapping that is needed?

Regards,
Steve.

entry.title: Re: B1SLayer: A clean and easy way to consume SAP Business One Service Layer with .NET
entry.content:
Good afternoon.
I am implementing the DLL in an API, but the question is whether there is a possibility of the sessionID being injected. For example, my APP is for managers to enter and see their documents so they can approve it. They log in with the SAP credentials in the Application and the sessionID saves it in memory and then when I want to do a PATCH it only sends me the sessionID and I do the PATCH but in this case the DLL I do not see an option that allows me to do it

entry.title: SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More)
entry.content:
This series of posts gives an overview of user experience innovations in the cloud from the last six months, covering our whole portfolio, in particular SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502. It also gives an overview of innovations in our UI technologies, making it ever easier to build and integrate UIs.

AI is revolutionizing user experience, giving users capabilities unheard of just a few years ago. SAP is harnessing the power of AI to help business users work more effectively: Joule is your copilot for seamless access to a variety of products a user needs, now covering 80% of the most-used transactions. Generative AI embedded in business applications gives users unprecedented insights and convenience. Beyond that, we continue to provide new applications with a great user experience, as well as UX-enhancing features.

How is AI, and especially generative AI, changing user experience for business users? Essentially via one or more of these patterns:

  • Summarizing business data in an easy to understand way – in text or graphical form.
  • Guiding and Recommending options or actions based on the current business context.
  • Creating proposals for texts or business data and entities such as assignments, performance goals with milestones etc., often based on natural language input.
  • Helping you write texts to people internally and externally, with our AI writing assistant.
  • Interacting via natural language with your application, e.g. search, filtering, analytical queries – as an alternative to established UI interactions.
  • Assisting you with all of the above, wherever you are, for all our cloud products – via Joule, your copilot for SAP systems.

Our overarching goal is to provide a great user experience everywhere. AI is a powerful means for achieving that, but of course you can also have a great user experience in applications without embedded AI.

A good user experience also needs to address teamwork, not just the experience of each individual – in other words, helping SAP users collaborate and work together.

I’ve collected examples from our latest releases to show how we are doing this – starting with our cross-product UX innovations in Joule and central entry points before looking at product UX innovations. Here is what the series will cover; as each part becomes available in the next couple of weeks I will update this list with links to each post:

  1. Central UX Services (this post)
    • Joule – latest UX innovations
    • Central entry points and services:
      • SAP Start / SAP Work Zone
      • SAP Task Center
      • SAP Mobile Start
  1. ERP: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 and SAP Fiori launchpad (AI, Joule and More).
  2. HR: SAP SuccessFactors (AI, Joule and More).
  3. Spend: SAP Ariba and SAP Fieldglass.
  4. Selling: SAP Customer Experience and SAP Industry Cloud – Retail.
  5. Platform: SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform.
  6. UI Design and Technology – web and mobile.

Before we get started: if you haven’t already seen them, do have a look at the overview of public cloud UX innovations I posted in February and July 2024:

  • SAP UX Innovations in Public Cloud Products, Joule, AI, Entry Points, Apple Vision Pro and more.
  • SAP UX Latest 2024 Innovations in Public Cloud: Joule, GenAI and More.

This series of posts includes Joule and AI UX highlights from the above products, but for a more comprehensive overview of our recent AI innovations and SAP’s Business AI strategy, with many UX examples, have a look at:

  • SAP Business AI: Release Highlights Q4 2024
  • White Paper: SAP Business AI.

Joule User Experience Innovations

Joule is the AI-powered copilot for SAP systems, offering a powerful new user experience, using natural language to access our cloud products and providing a seamless flow for the user.

Now, Joule is generally available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, as well as for our central entry points in the web SAP Start and SAP Work Zone (both standard and advanced editions). You can find examples in the respective product sections in this series; this section focusses on generic capabilities recently introduced or coming soon for Joule. If you want an overview of what’s new, or want to set up Joule in your landscape, have a look at the documentation:

  • What’s New for Joule.
  • Integrating Joule with SAP Solutions.

Joule already covers 80% of the most-used transactions in SAP systems and 1,200+ skills, tailored to business roles across SAP’s cloud portfolio such as hiring manager, billing specialist, procurement manager etc.

Joule Available on Mobile

Joule is also now generally available for mobile, running natively with SAP Mobile Start. Figure 1 shows examples of Joule running on a mobile phone.

Figure 1: Examples showing Joule integrated in SAP Mobile Start, showing from left to right: the start page, the informational pattern accessing SAP Help Portal, and the transactional pattern used to interact with SAP S/4HANA Cloud and then with SAP SuccessFactors. ALT Text: Four images are shown: the start page shows a “How can I help you” text, followed by some suggested prompts as buttons; the informational pattern shows the prompt “How can I customize a sales order”. Below that, the sixteen-line written answer is shown, followed by a “Search results” selector and three top search results. The navigational pattern shows the prompt “Show sales orders with delivery status not completed”, below that, the reply from Joule “Here’s what I’ve found” is followed by a card containing three lines and an “Open” button. A second transactional example with the prompt “Show profile of Maria Alvares”, followed by the result and a button for navigating to her profile.

On Apple devices you can now use Siri and Siri Shortcuts to seamlessly interact with Joule - which works even better with Apple Intelligence. Simply say "Hey Siri, ask SAP. Show my sales orders with delivery status not completed" and Joule will provide you with the respective content within the app.

Note that as of today, not all your prompts and web apps are supported on the phone; however, we are continuously adding further Joule business skills and scenarios.

Find out more here:

  • Joule on Mobile & Start Screen Personalization: SAP Mobile Start V2.1 - Release Update

Joule Analytical Pattern coming soon

In Q1/2025 we plan to provide a controlled release of the new analytical pattern for Joule, for those products listed above for which Joule is available. General availability currently planned for Q2/2025 (as usual for forward-looking statements, our plans are subject to change). It is powered by SAP Analytics Cloud, with data from models that are indexed by the Just Ask feature of SAP Analytics Cloud.

This means that Joule will soon support four interaction patterns, as shown in Figure 2.

  1. Informational pattern, for querying written documents, such as SAP documentation in the SAP Help Portal, or customer policies etc.
  2. Navigational pattern, for finding and navigating to applications.
  3. Transactional pattern, for interacting directly with business applicatinos
  4. Planned New: Analytical pattern, for answering analytical queries directly in Joule.

Figure 2: Joule Interaction Patterns. ALT text: four interaction patterns are shown: informational, with the prompt “what are the key rules for a team outing” followed by an eight-line answer and a link to a source document; navigational, showing a button “Go to Org Chart” after approving a position; transactional, with the text “Below are the new position details set to be hired starting today”, followed by a card summarizing the key details, and a prompt by the user “Send for approval”; Analytical, showing the prompt “What is the hiring versus attrition trend for this job role in the past 12 months?” and the answer shown as a chart with one line for hired and one for percentage attrition, plus numbers showing 124 similar roles hired and 6 role attrition.

Find out more about Joule for Analytics in this blog post:

  • Analytical Insights in Joule (powered by SAP Analytics Cloud), Controlled release in Q1 2025.

Outlook

We are working on making Joule available for further cloud products, as well as making it even more useful via agentic-AI, where Joule coordinates multi-agent systems. An AI agent can autonomously plan, reason, access external tools, work with other agents, and iteratively reflect on progress until it achieves its objective.

Joule will help SAP’s cloud users work faster, get instant insights, and achieve better outcomes by simply asking questions or telling the system what to do. Not only in English, but in their own language. We plan to allow users to interact with Joule in a growing number of languages including German, Spanish, French, and Portuguese. To get an idea of what is planned and what it could look like, accessing multiple products, using English and Spanish (with subtitles), have a look at this:

  • Video: AI is the new UI (3:00).

For more details of what is planned, have a look at the road map for Joule in the SAP Road Map Explorer:

  • Joule Road Map.

Central entry points and services

SAP Start

The integration with Joule in SAP Start is now generally available. The purpose of SAP Start is to bring together relevant business information from SAP for each user in one central place, and also to provide the one place to go to start your SAP applications in the cloud. SAP Start:

  • Gives you quick access to all your applications via the simple yet powerful search field;
  • New: Gives you direct access to your product home pages, as shown in Figure 3.
  • Proactively informs you about your to-dos from all products which are connected to the SAP Task Center;
  • Gives you key insights via insights cards from products connected to SAP Start,
  • Assists you with Joule, your copilot for working with SAP.

Figure 3: SAP Start, now with direct access to product home pages. This example shows direct access to three product home pages in the “Products” section: SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP Fieldglass and SAP SuccessFactors. ALT Text: Below the “Products” section the screen shows the To-Dos section, with four cards for pending tasks, the first three with Approve and Reject buttons on them. Below that, the Insights section is shown, with two rows of four cards, starting with lists of My Statements of Work, My Workers, My Team, List of Top 4 Customer Projects.

Now, SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Integrated Business Planning support SAP Start, in addition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP SuccessFactors and SAP Fieldglass. Other cloud products can be integrated to provide tasks via SAP Task Center. You can find out how to set up and integrate cloud products to SAP Start here:

  • Documentation: SAP Start.

SAP Build Work Zone

Joule is now also generally available for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition:

  • Joule is Now Integrated into SAP Build Work Zone!

SAP Build Work Zone now allows you to integrate web UIs based on HTML5 from other SAP BTP subaccounts. This is done by creating an HTML5 business solution that acts as a content provider in the subaccount.

  • This business solution can be accessed not only by the local subaccount but also by remote subaccounts through destinations.
  • To set up an HTML5 business solution as a content provider, the provider subaccount needs to adjust the HTML5 code to include a cdm.json file, which defines the design-time business content such as roles, groups, spaces, and pages, along with destinations. Find out more here:
    • Simplifying HTML5 Content Consumption Across Subaccounts.

In my blog post from February 2024, I explained that SAP Build Work Zone allows customers to define guided experiences, i.e. wizards, to guide users step-by-step through complex processes.

A major enhancement in the October 2024 release is the ability to model guided processes directly in SAP Build Process Automation, bringing powerful process automation to your SAP Build projects. Get an overview here:

  • Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build.

Just recently, a new wizard setting was added to allow the execution of the same wizard multiple times. This setting is available both in freestyle wizards and in wizards that are based on a Guided Process from SAP Build Process Automation. For example, an admin can enable this option on the Purchase Order wizard, and this will allow users to run this wizard multiple times to purchase different items.

Enhanced Page Builder Experience across SAP Build Work Zone:

The SAP Build Work Zone page builder experience has been significantly enhanced with new features. Users can now enjoy a widget context menu, the ability to move sections up and down, and a section configuration menu, as shown in Figure 4. Additionally, the option to show or hide section headers and various accessibility improvements make the editor more user-friendly and versatile.

Figure 4: SAP Build Work Zone enhanced page builder experience. ALT Text: the image shows a section of the Work Zone screen with a visual editor. The section is partitioned into three sub-sections, each with a button “Add Widget”, the rightmost one shows an open context menu for the button, which shows that you can also add a column. The leftmost section contains a simple list of items, the middle one a bar chart for Project Cloud Revenue, the rightmost a donut chart for Open SAP Incidents.

Here is the guide with recommendations and further details:

  • How to Build and Design a Workpage.

SAP Task Center

Users can conveniently access tasks from many different systems in one place – not only from SAP products, we also provide APIs for integrating tasks from 3rd-party products.

Even more conveniently, users can now be notified of new tasks via e-mail. Also, end user notifications can now be enabled if you are using SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition as central point of entry for accessing your business applications.

SAP Build Work Zone itself can also be set up as a task provider for SAP Task Center. This means that tasks created by SAP Build Work Zone will also appear in SAP Task Center.

Furthermore, SAP Task Center now allows users to forward approvals.

New Intelligent Recommendations Feature Available for SAP Build Process Automation Tasks

We are introducing AI to expedite task processing: intelligent recommendations leverage artificial intelligence to streamline and accelerate task approval processes. They assist users in making decisions by analyzing key attributes relevant to specific business cases and generating a confidence level in percent for their tasks. Figure 5 shows some approvals with a confidence level of 100%, and some with 97.5%.

Figure 5: New Intelligent Recommendations feature in SAP Task Center. ALT Text: An image of the task center list of tasks, with a filter bar at the top with seven fields available, and the field “Task Type” set to “Manager Approval”. The list shows six entries. The new column “Confidence Level” is highlighted, and shows 100% for the first four entries, and 97.5% for the last two.

Find out more here:

  • Enhance Task Approvals with the Power of AI.

SAP Mobile Start

Beyond providing Joule natively for mobile devices, SAP Mobile Start has recently introduced a number of user experience improvements with their 2.0 and 2.1 releases. Here, I would like to highlight the support for ‘new site experience’, giving users a more structured view of applications and information.

Figure 6: SAP Mobile Start with Spaces & Pages from new site experience. ALT Text: Two images of mobile phones. On the left the Apps page, which contains “My Apps Space” showing a list of three page names “Customer Management Page”, “Project management page” and “App Page”. On the right, the App Page is shown, with a list of six apps as cards, with an icon on the left of the app name. The first three (My Inbox, Monitor Situations, Manage My Timesheet) have coloured KPI values displayed on the right.

A further significant enhancement is the support of declarative UI integration cards, which are displayed embedded in the respective pages on the Apps screen.

Figure 7: SAP Mobile Start supports UI integration cards from SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. ALT Text: Two mobile phone images. On the left Project Apps, which shows a flat card for Project Factsheet and below that a UI integration card showing overall status for active projects, with a list of five projects along with their overall status – four on track, one with “high risk” in red; at the bottom of the card a “See all” button. On the right two further UI integration cards are shown: a list of top 4 projects, and “Cost to Date – Timeline” containing a KPI number for the actual cost to date and below that a line chart of actual and planned cost per month.

For a good and satisfying user experience, being able to personalize the UI so that it fits with how you like to work is a key element. To this end, SAP Mobile Start has now introduced personalization of the start page, allowing you to rearrange the order of sections as well as hiding sections which you don’t need – take a look at it in Figure 8.

Figure 8: SAP Mobile Start personalization of the start screen. ALT Text: Four mobile phone images. On the left the start page, with the sections Insights and News visible. Next, an image of the edit screen, with five sections listed: Latest To-Dos, Favorites, Insights, App Suggestions (deselected), News. Next, an image of the five sections, all selected, now with Favorites first and Latest To-Dos second. On the right, the start page with the sections Favorites at the top (containing flat cards for apps), below that Latest To-Dos (containing larger cards for ToDos, one shocing a purchase requisition with Total Amount $799.99).

To find out more about recent innovations as well as what is planned in our road map, have a look at:

  • SAP Mobile Start V2.0 - Release Update
  • Joule on Mobile & Start Screen Personalization: SAP Mobile Start V2.1 - Release Update
  • SAP Mobile Start Road Map.

Continued soon…

I hope you enjoyed this first part, covering cross-product UX services. I will post part two in the next few days, covering a a large number of UX innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 and SAP Fiori launchpad (AI, Joule and More).

entry.title: Leveraging API Traffic Predictions in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's fast-paced digital landscape, efficiently managing API traffic and predicting future trends are crucial for maintaining seamless operations. SAP Integration Suite introduces a powerful feature called Predictions, which leverages AI to forecast future API call volumes based on historical data. This blog post will guide you through understanding, enabling, and viewing predictions, helping you optimize your API strategy and make informed decisions.

 

Key Use Cases

Traffic Predictions:

    • As an API Owner, you can see the API traffic prediction for all your deployed APIs for any duration in the next three months. They can further be aggregated to see the data day-wise, week-wise or month-wise.
    • Forecasting the API traffic during a specific time period enables planning ahead on resource allocation, optimizing costs, improving performance, reliability, and scalability. For example, a travel booking site might predict increased API traffic during holiday seasons. This foresight allows the site to scale up its infrastructure in advance, ensuring it can handle the higher demand without performance issues.

Weekly Trends:

    • As an API Owner, you can see the typical weekly trend of API traffic in your tenant.
    • Weekly trends help identify API traffic patterns that occur within a week, aiding decision-making. For example, an e-commerce platform might notice higher API traffic on weekends due to increased shopping activity. This insight allows the platform to allocate more resources during weekends to handle the surge in traffic and ensure a smooth user experience.

Daily Trends:

    • As an API Owner, you can see the typical daily trend of API traffic in your tenant.
    • Daily trends help identify API traffic patterns during a 24-hour period and enable decision-making. For instance, a food delivery service might observe peak API traffic during lunch and dinner hours. By identifying these peak hours, the service can avoid rolling out updates during these times to minimize disruption and ensure timely deliveries.

 

How does it work?  

The Predictions feature in SAP Integration Suite analyzes past API call data to forecast future traffic volumes. By identifying trends, it provides insights into expected traffic patterns, helping API owners make informed decisions.

 

Enabling Predictions

As an Administrator of a specific Integration Suite - API Management tenant, one could decide to turn the feature on from the Settings -> APIs page of the Integration Suite application with a simple toggle switch. The toggle button activates both Anomaly Detection and Predictions for API calls.

Note: The feature is in the process of getting updated in our global Data centres. Check this Note for information about regional availability.

Note: The availability of the feature is dependent on your SAP Integration Suite service plan. Check this Note for information about the various plans and supported features.

 

Viewing Predictions and Traffic Trends

Once enabled, you can view API call predictions and trends in the analytics dashboard under a separate tab called Predictions. It offers a visualization of expected traffic patterns over a specified timeframe. You can customize the duration and frequency of predictions to suit your needs.

 

Key Features

  • API Call Predictions: Analyze future API call traffic for specific timeframes such as daily, weekly, or monthly.
  • API Call Trends: Examine the frequency of API calls within set timeframes, such as hourly or daily.
  • Customizable Options: Select specific API proxies, choose the prediction duration, and set the frequency according to your preferences.

 

Benefits of Using Predictions

By leveraging the Predictions feature, you can:

  • Optimize Resource Allocation: Ensure your resources are allocated efficiently based on expected traffic.
  • Manage System Load: Anticipate high traffic periods and prepare your systems accordingly.
  • Make Informed Decisions: Use insights from traffic trends to refine your API strategy and improve overall performance.

 

Conclusion

The Predictions feature in SAP Integration Suite is a valuable tool for any organization looking to enhance its API management capabilities. By forecasting future API call volumes and identifying traffic trends, you can optimize resource allocation, manage system load, and make informed decisions to drive your business forward.
